project_summary:
Presentation-layer redesign of Aitri Hub: transforms the existing local monitoring dashboard (FR-001..009 already shipped) into a signals-first mission-control interface plus an in-Hub QA workspace, grounded in the UI Discovery Brief (feature_context/aitri-hub-ui-discovery.md), the two interaction prototypes (aitri-hub-jarvis-health.jsx = monitor/health/sessions authority, aitri-hub-final.jsx = artifact-tree authority), and the parent UI_UX_SPEC_V2.md (design system + data contracts). This feature is UI/UX only — the Hub's professionalization (installable app, isolated runtime, architecture upgrade) is deferred to a separate future feature (aitri-hub-platform-upgrade).

Product Analysis:
  North Star KPI: Time-to-urgency — from opening the Monitor, a user identifies the single most urgent project in <10 seconds (via bento size, ordering, and color, without reading body text).
  JTBD: 'When I oversee several Aitri projects across Dev/Product/QA, help me see which one needs attention and act on it — read its full state and, for QA, run a test — without opening another tool.'
  Guardrail metric: Data ingestion parity — the redesign must not change the data the Hub reads from Aitri projects (same dashboard.json contract, same monitor collection), and each detail section must render in ≤2s for a project with up to 20 artifacts.

Scope note: this is a change to an existing system. New FRs (FR-010+) cover the redesigned surfaces; the existing collection/admin/alerts engine (FR-001..009) and the shipped qa-workspace feature are the substrate the redesign re-presents and must not break (see Regression NFRs).
no_go_zone: No role/user separation or differentiated access — the three roles (Dev, Product, QA) share one unsegmented view in v1
No push or background notifications, and no toasts — alerting is in-page only (parent Annex A cut)
No artifact editing from Hub — the reader can approve/reject/complete via existing actions, but never edits artifact content
No file export (PDF/Markdown) — browser print of the report view is the only output; no server-side export
No support for non-Aitri projects — a directory without Aitri state is not rendered
No authentication, sessions, or multi-tenant access control in v1
No aitri command execution beyond the already-shipped approve/reject/complete artifact actions — the Hub does not run run-phase/verify from the UI

Deferred to a separate feature (aitri-hub-platform-upgrade): making the Hub installable, giving it an isolated runtime/environment, and the general architecture upgrade — explicitly NOT in this v0.3.0

Cut from v1 (parent UI_UX_SPEC_V2 Annex A): team/commit velocity metric, vulnerable-dependency alerts, code-coverage-% surfacing (FR coverage only, unless a coverage quality-gate is declared), light theme, uptime
No mobile/tablet-optimized layout and no native mobile build — desktop web only; small viewports may degrade without breaking
No manual refresh control in v1 — data syncs on a periodic timer only
No separate 'release' entity or release CRUD — a manual test execution binds to the current Aitri verify-run/snapshot, not a user-managed release object

user_personas.goal: Spot which Aitri project is blocked, inspect drift/verify/artifacts, and resume work — without cd-ing into repos or reading raw JSON
user_personas.pain_point: Today must open each repo, read raw artifacts, and run terminal commands; cannot see at a glance which project is critical vs merely idle

user_personas.goal: Follow the phase pipeline and read artifacts in product language (PRD/TRD/QA Plan) to validate overall project state
user_personas.pain_point: Aitri's technical file names and raw Markdown/JSON are unreadable for a non-CLI role; visual artifact content is buried

user_personas.goal: Use Hub as an active work tool — consult test cases, record manual test executions with evidence, review bugs, and produce a quality report
user_personas.pain_point: No place to register manual test results tied to the project's verify run, or to see test cases and bugs alongside the project state

functional_requirements.title: Monitor — urgency-weighted bento grid with worst-first ordering and health filters
functional_requirements.description: Redesign the Monitor (home) as a bento grid where each project's card size encodes its computed health urgency: CRITICAL = large (spans 2 columns) with alert glow + pulse animation, AT RISK = medium, NOMINAL = compact/neutral. Cards are ordered worst-first (CRITICAL → AT RISK → NOMINAL) and re-sorted on each data refresh. A filter bar (ALL / CRITICAL / AT RISK / NOMINAL) narrows the grid, and a topbar shows a radial NOMINAL-n/total summary, per-state counts, a sync-countdown ticker, and a clock. Grid columns follow the parent spec breakpoints (≥1200px = 4-col, 768–1200px = 2-col).
acceptance_criteria: Given a project computed CRITICAL, when the Monitor renders, then its card spans 2 grid columns and shows an alert glow with a pulse animation completing a cycle in ≤1500ms
Given projects of mixed health, when the grid renders, then order is strictly CRITICAL first then AT RISK then NOMINAL, and re-sorts within ≤1 refresh cycle when a project's health changes
Given the filter set to CRITICAL, when applied, then only CRITICAL cards are shown and the count in the bar equals the number of CRITICAL projects
Given a viewport ≥1200px the grid renders 4 columns and at 768–1200px it renders 2 columns with no horizontal scroll
Given zero registered projects, when the Monitor loads, then the existing onboarding empty state (FR-005) renders — the redesigned grid never shows a broken/blank layout
Every health state is distinguishable without color alone — each card carries a text label (NOMINAL/AT RISK/CRITICAL) and an icon, contrast ≥4.5:1

functional_requirements.title: Monitor project card — health badge, segmented pipeline, 6 signal tiles, top issue, detail CTA
functional_requirements.description: Each Monitor card presents, without requiring the user to read prose: the project's readable name (not the technical folder name), a colored health badge, the phase pipeline as a segmented bar (no phase text), 6 compact signal tiles with semantic color — Tests, Drift, Verify, Pending commits, External signals, Rejections — the single most-critical active issue on one line, and a button into the project detail.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project, when its card renders, then it shows the readable project name and a health badge whose color matches its computed state
Given a project with N approved phases, when the card renders, then the segmented pipeline bar fills exactly N of 5 segments in the phase status colors
Given a project, when the card renders, then exactly 6 signal tiles appear (Tests, Drift, Verify, Pending, Signals, Rejections), each colored green/amber/red by its own threshold

Given a project with ≥1 active issue, when the card renders, then the single highest-severity issue is shown on one line; given no active issue, then that line shows a nominal/all-clear state instead of empty space
Given a card, when the detail CTA is activated, then the Project Detail view for that project opens
Given a tile whose underlying datum is unavailable (e.g. no verify run), when the card renders, then the tile shows an explicit 'N/A' rather than a misleading zero

functional_requirements.title: Project Detail shell — single-page, fixed sidebar + scrollable content, browser-back to Monitor
functional_requirements.description: Opening any card routes to a single-page Project Detail with a two-column layout: a fixed left sidebar (project name + health, active branch and type local/remote, mini phase pipeline, section nav with per-section issue-count badges, quick stats: active issues / rejections / drift / tests) and a scrollable right content area hosting the sections (Overview, Health, Artifacts, Sessions, Alerts, plus the QA sections). Navigation is single-page; the browser Back button returns from detail to the Monitor with no full reload.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Project Detail is open, when the user presses browser Back, then the app returns to the Monitor without a full page reload and preserves the prior filter
Given a section with active issues, when the sidebar renders, then that section's nav item shows a count badge equal to its issue count
Given the detail view, when it renders, then the sidebar stays fixed while the content column scrolls independently
Given a project id that no longer exists in the snapshot, when its detail is requested, then an explicit 'project not found / removed' state renders instead of a crash or blank page
Given the sidebar quick-stats, when rendered, then active-issues / rejections / drift / tests values match the same values shown on the project's Monitor card
Given a viewport ≥1200px, when a nav section is switched, then the content swaps in ≤200ms while the sidebar stays fixed, and all sidebar text meets contrast ≥4.5:1

functional_requirements.title: Detail — Overview section
functional_requirements.description: Executive summary of the project: description; phase-by-phase pipeline where each phase shows its visual state (approved, completed-pending-approval, active, future) with a readable label (not the technical file name); key metric tiles (last session, agent, branch, verify, pending commits, version); and test telemetry (passed, failing, skipped, total, age of last run) with a circular gauge.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project, when Overview renders, then all 5 phases appear each labelled with its state (approved / completed-pending / active / future) using readable labels, not technical file names
Given a project with a verify summary, when Overview renders, then passed/failing/skipped/total and last-run age are shown with a circular gauge reflecting the pass ratio
Given a project with no verify run yet, when Overview renders, then the test-telemetry block shows an explicit 'no run yet' state rather than a zeroed gauge
Given the metric tiles, when rendered, then last session, agent, branch, verify, pending commits, and version each display the project's actual value

functional_requirements.title: Detail — Health section with five diagnostic dimensions
functional_requirements.description: A diagnostic split into five dimensions in fixed order — Pipeline (phases completed-not-approved, phase drift), Tests (failing, stale, verify not run), Code (unpushed commits, off-pipeline changes), Artifacts (unapproved, rejections with feedback), Version (project vs installed CLI mismatch). Each dimension is a panel with an OK / WARN / CRITICAL badge and, per issue, the issue text and its available remediation action.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project, when Health renders, then exactly five dimension panels appear in order Pipeline, Tests, Code, Artifacts, Version, each with an OK/WARN/CRITICAL badge
Given a dimension with ≥1 issue, when rendered, then each issue lists its message and the remediation action available for it
Given a dimension with no issues, when rendered, then it shows an explicit 'all checks passing' state, not an empty panel
Given a project with a version mismatch (project vs installed CLI), when Health renders, then the Version dimension is WARN or CRITICAL and names the two versions
Given a dimension badge, when rendered, then its level is conveyed by label+icon (not color alone) with contrast ≥4.5:1

functional_requirements.title: Detail — Artifacts explorer tree
functional_requirements.description: An artifact explorer with a file tree on the left grouped by phase. Each phase folder shows a rolled-up status glyph (✓ all approved, ○ any pending, ✕ any rejected). Each file shows its readable product name (per FR-019), size, and age, with the technical file name present as secondary reference and a per-file status chip. Selecting a file opens it in the reader panel (FR-016); re-selecting toggles it closed.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project's artifacts, when the tree renders, then files are grouped under their phase folder and each folder shows a rolled-up glyph (✓/○/✕) matching its children's worst status
Given a file row, when rendered, then it shows the readable product name as the title and the technical file name as secondary text, plus size and age
Given a file is selected, when clicked again, then the reader panel closes (toggle) and no file is marked active
Given a phase with no artifacts yet, when the tree renders, then that phase shows an explicit empty/pending state rather than being silently omitted
Given the reader is not yet opened, when the explorer first renders, then the reader panel shows a 'select a file to read' empty state
Given a project with ≤20 artifacts, when the explorer opens, then the full tree renders in ≤2s and status glyphs/labels meet contrast ≥4.5:1

functional_requirements.title: Detail — Artifact reader renders Markdown, JSON, and embedded images
functional_requirements.description: The reader panel renders the selected artifact's real content: Markdown is rendered formatted (not raw); JSON is rendered as a readable structured projection (not raw text); embedded images referenced in Markdown — by a relative path within the artifact's spec folder or as a base64 data-URI — are rendered inline, supporting PNG, JPG, SVG, GIF, and WebP. The reader also shows the artifact status, any rejection feedback, and the status-appropriate action (approve / reject-with-feedback / complete).
acceptance_criteria: Given a Markdown artifact, when opened, then it renders formatted (headings, lists, tables, code) with no raw Markdown syntax visible
Given a JSON artifact, when opened, then it renders as a structured human-readable projection, not a raw text dump
Given a Markdown artifact embedding an image by relative path or data-URI in PNG/JPG/SVG/GIF/WebP, when opened, then the image renders inline

Given a Markdown artifact referencing an image that cannot be resolved (missing file / unsupported type), when opened, then a placeholder with the alt text renders and the rest of the document still renders — no crash
Given a rejected artifact, when opened, then the reader shows the rejection feedback and offers the reject/approve actions appropriate to its status
Given an artifact ≤1MB of text, when opened, then the reader renders it in ≤2s

functional_requirements.title: Detail — Sessions timeline
functional_requirements.description: A chronological log of the project's lifecycle events (approvals, rejections, completions, phase starts), newest first. Each event shows relative time, an event type with color+icon, the phase, and inline rejection feedback when present. Below the timeline, a last-session context panel shows the description of work done, the files touched, and the agent.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with lifecycle events, when Sessions renders, then events appear newest-first with relative time, typed icon+label, and phase
Given a rejection event with feedback, when rendered, then the feedback text is shown inline on that event
Given a project with no events yet, when Sessions renders, then an explicit empty state is shown rather than a blank panel
Given last-session context exists, when rendered, then the work description, files-touched chips, and agent are shown

functional_requirements.title: Detail — Alerts aggregation
functional_requirements.description: All active issues in one place: the issues from the five Health dimensions (each with dimension label and WARN/CRITICAL level) plus external tool signals (e.g. npm-audit, eslint) with tool, type, severity, message, and remediation command. When there are no alerts, an explicit all-clear confirmation state is shown (not merely absence of content).
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with Health issues and external signals, when Alerts renders, then each is a card showing its dimension/tool label, level/severity, message, and (for signals) the remediation command
Given a project with zero alerts, when Alerts renders, then an explicit all-clear confirmation state is shown, not a blank panel
Given an alert card, when rendered, then its severity is conveyed by label+icon (not color alone) with contrast ≥4.5:1
Given the Alerts count, when rendered, then it equals the sum of Health issues plus external signals shown in the sidebar badge

functional_requirements.title: Artifact name translation — fixed technical→product mapping, features inherit with prefix
functional_requirements.description: Everywhere an artifact is named, Hub shows a readable product name via a fixed built-in mapping (01_REQUIREMENTS.json → 'PRD — Product Requirements', 02_SYSTEM_DESIGN.md → 'TRD — Technical Design', 03_TEST_CASES.json → 'QA Plan — Test Cases', 04_IMPLEMENTATION_MANIFEST.json → 'Implementation Manifest', 05_PROOF_OF_COMPLIANCE.json → 'Release Compliance', 06_EXTERNAL_SIGNALS.json → 'External Signals'). Feature artifacts inherit the same mapping prefixed by the feature name. The technical file name remains visible as secondary reference (tooltip / reader metadata). The mapping is fixed in v1 (not user-configurable).
acceptance_criteria: Given any of the six known technical artifact names, when displayed anywhere in Hub, then its fixed product name is shown as the primary label
Given a feature artifact (under features/<name>/), when displayed, then its product name is prefixed with the feature name and the same base mapping applies
Given a translated artifact, when the user hovers or opens the reader, then the original technical file name is visible as secondary reference
Given an artifact whose technical name is not in the mapping, when displayed, then it falls back to showing the raw file name (no crash, no blank label)

Given a translated artifact row, when rendered, then the product name is the primary label at contrast ≥4.5:1 and the secondary technical name renders at a smaller size (≥11px) without truncating the product name

functional_requirements.title: QA Workspace — Test Cases view
functional_requirements.description: Within Project Detail, a QA section listing all test cases of the project organized by phase and feature, sourced from Aitri's testing-phase artifacts (03_TEST_CASES.json and feature equivalents). Each case shows ID, description, type (manual/automated), and status (pending/passed/failed/blocked). Filters by phase, feature, status, and type. Manual test cases can have their status edited directly from Hub.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with testing artifacts, when Test Cases renders, then every case appears grouped by phase and feature with ID, description, type, and status
Given the type filter set to manual, when applied, then only manual cases are listed and the count reflects the filtered set
Given a manual test case, when its status is edited in Hub, then the new status persists and is reflected on next render
Given an attempt to edit the status of an automated test case, when made, then Hub rejects/blocks the edit (automated statuses come from runs, not manual edits)
Given a project with no testing artifacts, when Test Cases renders, then an explicit empty state explains none exist yet

functional_requirements.title: QA Workspace — record manual test execution bound to the current verify run
functional_requirements.description: QA records manual test results: select a test case, then capture per-execution fields — result (passed/failed/blocked), notes, attached evidence (image/screenshot), and test environment. Each execution is associated with the project's current Aitri verify-run/snapshot (resultsBinding) and appended to the case's history, so a case can have multiple executions over time. No separate release entity is created in v1.
acceptance_criteria: Given a selected test case, when QA submits an execution with result/notes/evidence/environment, then it is stored bound to the current verify-run id and appears in that case's history
Given a case with prior executions, when a new execution is recorded, then the history retains all executions in chronological order (execution is additive, never overwrites)
Given a recorded execution, when the Hub process restarts, then the execution and its evidence reference survive (persisted under ~/.aitri-hub/)
Given an execution submitted with no result selected, when submitted, then it is rejected with a validation message and nothing is persisted
Given evidence of a disallowed type or over the size cap, when attached, then it is rejected before persistence (see NFR-010)

functional_requirements.title: QA Workspace — Bugs view
functional_requirements.description: A list of bugs reported in the project, consumed from Aitri project files (BUGS.json and feature equivalents) — bugs live in Aitri, not an external tracker. Each bug shows ID, description, severity, phase where detected, and status. Filters by severity, status, and feature. A per-bug detail shows full description, reproduction steps, attached evidence, and status-change history. Bug parse errors are surfaced explicitly (never a silent '0 bugs').
acceptance_criteria: Given a project with a BUGS.json, when Bugs renders, then each bug shows ID, description, severity, detected-phase, and status
Given the severity filter, when set, then only bugs of that severity are listed and the count reflects the filtered set
Given a bug, when its detail opens, then full description, reproduction steps, evidence, and status-change history are shown
Given a BUGS.json that is missing, when Bugs renders, then an explicit empty state is shown; given a BUGS.json that fails to parse, then a parse-error warning is surfaced rather than a silent zero
Given bugs across product and features, when rendered, then the feature filter scopes the list to the selected feature

functional_requirements.title: QA Workspace — quality reports (project / feature / release), on-demand and printable
functional_requirements.description: Generate and view quality reports in three scopes: Project summary (overall state, test coverage, bugs by severity, cases passed/failed/pending), Per-feature (a feature's cases, results, associated bugs), and Release report (all of the above scoped to a specific verify-run/iteration). Reports render live on-demand from the current snapshot inside Hub with a clean, legible layout, and offer a browser-print-optimized view (PDF via browser print). No file export is produced.
acceptance_criteria: Given a project, when the Project-summary report is opened, then it renders on-demand from the current snapshot with overall state, test coverage, bugs-by-severity, and case pass/fail/pending counts
Given a feature is selected, when the Per-feature report is opened, then it is scoped to that feature's cases, results, and bugs
Given a verify-run/iteration is selected, when the Release report is opened, then its figures are scoped to that run
Given a report is open, when the user invokes browser print, then a print-optimized layout renders (no navigation chrome, legible on A4/Letter)
Given a scope with no data (e.g. a feature with no test cases), when its report is opened, then an explicit empty report state is shown rather than a broken layout

functional_requirements.title: Global — periodic data sync with countdown ticker, no manual refresh
functional_requirements.description: Hub data syncs on a periodic timer (reading the current snapshot); the topbar shows a countdown ticker to the next refresh. There is no manual refresh control in v1. On each refresh, the Monitor re-sorts and re-sizes cards and all open sections reflect the new snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Monitor is open, when the refresh timer elapses, then the ticker resets and the grid re-sorts/re-sizes from the new snapshot without a full page reload
Given no manual refresh control exists, when the user inspects the UI, then there is no refresh button anywhere in v1
Given a snapshot read fails or is stale on a refresh, when it occurs, then the last-known data remains visible with an explicit 'snapshot stale' indicator rather than a blank screen

user_stories.i_want: the Monitor to size and order projects by urgency
user_stories.so_that: I spot the most critical project in seconds without reading each card

acceptance_criteria.given: 5 projects where exactly one is CRITICAL and two are AT RISK
acceptance_criteria.then: the CRITICAL card is largest (spans 2 columns) and appears first, before the AT RISK and NOMINAL cards

acceptance_criteria.given: the Monitor is showing all projects
acceptance_criteria.when: the user selects the CRITICAL filter
acceptance_criteria.then: only CRITICAL cards remain and the bar count equals the number of CRITICAL projects

acceptance_criteria.given: a project's health changes from CRITICAL to NOMINAL between refreshes
acceptance_criteria.then: its card shrinks to compact and moves to the end of the order within one refresh cycle

user_stories.i_want: each card to show the health badge, pipeline, six signals and the top issue
user_stories.so_that: I understand a project's state without opening it or reading raw data

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with 3 of 5 phases approved
acceptance_criteria.then: the segmented pipeline fills exactly 3 of 5 segments and 6 signal tiles are shown

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with no verify run
acceptance_criteria.then: the Verify/Tests tiles show 'N/A' rather than a green or zero value

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with an active critical issue
acceptance_criteria.then: the single highest-severity issue is shown on one line

user_stories.i_want: a single-page detail with a fixed sidebar and browser-back to the monitor
user_stories.so_that: I can navigate a project and return without losing my place

acceptance_criteria.given: a Project Detail is open having arrived from the CRITICAL filter
acceptance_criteria.then: the Monitor reappears with the CRITICAL filter still applied and no full reload

acceptance_criteria.given: a project whose Health has 2 issues
acceptance_criteria.then: the Health nav item shows a badge of 2

acceptance_criteria.given: a project id that was removed from the snapshot
acceptance_criteria.then: a 'project not found' state renders instead of a crash

user_stories.i_want: an overview with the phase pipeline in readable labels and test telemetry
user_stories.so_that: I can validate where the project stands without technical file names

acceptance_criteria.given: a project mid-pipeline
acceptance_criteria.then: each of the 5 phases shows a readable label and its state (approved/completed-pending/active/future)

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with a verify summary of 27 passed / 0 failed
acceptance_criteria.then: the gauge reflects a full pass ratio and shows passed/failing/skipped/total plus last-run age

acceptance_criteria.given: a project that has never run verify
acceptance_criteria.then: the telemetry block shows an explicit 'no run yet' state, not a zeroed gauge

user_stories.i_want: a Health section split into five dimensions with remediation
user_stories.so_that: I know exactly what is wrong and what to run to fix it

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with a failing test and a version mismatch
acceptance_criteria.then: the Tests dimension is CRITICAL/WARN and the Version dimension names both versions

acceptance_criteria.given: a dimension with an issue that has a known remediation
acceptance_criteria.then: the remediation action/command is shown with the issue

acceptance_criteria.given: a dimension with no issues
acceptance_criteria.then: it shows 'all checks passing' rather than an empty panel

user_stories.i_want: an artifact tree grouped by phase with rolled-up status
user_stories.so_that: I can find and open the artifact I need at a glance

acceptance_criteria.given: a phase whose artifacts are all approved
acceptance_criteria.then: the phase folder shows a ✓ glyph; if any child is rejected it shows ✕

acceptance_criteria.given: a selected file
acceptance_criteria.when: the same file row is clicked again
acceptance_criteria.then: the reader closes and no file is active

acceptance_criteria.given: the explorer just opened with nothing selected
acceptance_criteria.then: the reader shows a 'select a file to read' empty state

user_stories.i_want: artifacts rendered as formatted docs, structured JSON and inline images
user_stories.so_that: I never have to read raw JSON or Markdown to understand an artifact

acceptance_criteria.given: a Markdown artifact with headings, a table and an embedded PNG by relative path
acceptance_criteria.then: it renders formatted with the image inline and no raw Markdown syntax visible

acceptance_criteria.given: a JSON artifact
acceptance_criteria.then: it renders as a structured readable projection, not a raw text dump

acceptance_criteria.given: a Markdown artifact referencing a missing or unsupported image
acceptance_criteria.then: a placeholder with alt text renders and the rest of the document still renders without crashing

user_stories.i_want: a chronological sessions timeline with last-session context
user_stories.so_that: I can see what happened last and resume the project

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with approval and rejection events
acceptance_criteria.then: events appear newest-first with typed icon+label and phase, and rejection feedback is shown inline

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with no events
acceptance_criteria.then: an explicit empty state is shown

acceptance_criteria.given: last-session context exists
acceptance_criteria.then: the work description, files-touched chips and agent are shown

user_stories.i_want: one Alerts view combining health issues and external signals
user_stories.so_that: I have a single actionable list of everything wrong

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with 2 health issues and 1 npm-audit signal
acceptance_criteria.then: 3 cards appear, the signal card showing its remediation command

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with zero alerts
acceptance_criteria.then: an explicit all-clear confirmation state is shown, not a blank panel

user_stories.i_want: technical artifact names shown as product names
user_stories.so_that: I read PRD/TRD/QA Plan instead of file names

acceptance_criteria.given: an artifact named 01_REQUIREMENTS.json
acceptance_criteria.then: its primary label reads 'PRD — Product Requirements' and the technical name is secondary

acceptance_criteria.given: a feature artifact under features/redesign/
acceptance_criteria.then: its product name is prefixed with the feature name

acceptance_criteria.given: an artifact whose name is not in the mapping
acceptance_criteria.then: it falls back to the raw file name without crashing or showing a blank label

user_stories.i_want: to see all test cases by phase and feature and edit manual statuses
user_stories.so_that: I can track testing without leaving Hub

acceptance_criteria.given: a project with manual and automated cases
acceptance_criteria.when: the manual type filter is applied
acceptance_criteria.then: only manual cases are listed with the correct count

acceptance_criteria.given: a manual case
acceptance_criteria.when: its status is changed in Hub
acceptance_criteria.then: the new status persists and shows on next render

acceptance_criteria.given: an automated case
acceptance_criteria.when: a manual status edit is attempted
acceptance_criteria.then: Hub blocks the edit

user_stories.i_want: to record a manual test execution with evidence bound to the verify run
user_stories.so_that: results are traceable to the run they were tested against

acceptance_criteria.given: a selected case and a current verify-run
acceptance_criteria.when: QA submits result=passed with notes, environment and a PNG screenshot
acceptance_criteria.then: the execution persists bound to that verify-run id and appears in the case history

acceptance_criteria.given: a case with one prior execution
acceptance_criteria.then: both executions remain in chronological history (additive, no overwrite) and survive a Hub restart

acceptance_criteria.given: an execution form with no result selected
acceptance_criteria.then: it is rejected with a validation message and nothing is persisted

user_stories.i_want: to review bugs read from Aitri with filters and detail
user_stories.so_that: I can triage without an external tracker

acceptance_criteria.given: a project BUGS.json with bugs of mixed severity
acceptance_criteria.then: only high-severity bugs are listed with the correct count

acceptance_criteria.given: a bug
acceptance_criteria.then: full description, reproduction steps, evidence and status history are shown

acceptance_criteria.given: a BUGS.json that fails to parse
acceptance_criteria.then: a parse-error warning is surfaced instead of a silent '0 bugs'

user_stories.i_want: on-demand quality reports I can print
user_stories.so_that: I can share a project/feature/release quality summary

acceptance_criteria.given: a project
acceptance_criteria.then: it renders on-demand from the current snapshot with coverage, bugs-by-severity and case pass/fail/pending counts

acceptance_criteria.given: an open report
acceptance_criteria.then: a print-optimized layout renders without navigation chrome

acceptance_criteria.given: a feature with no test cases
acceptance_criteria.then: an explicit empty report state is shown

user_stories.i_want: the dashboard to refresh on a timer with a countdown
user_stories.so_that: I always see current state without a manual refresh

acceptance_criteria.given: the Monitor is open
acceptance_criteria.then: the ticker resets and the grid re-sorts from the new snapshot without a full reload

acceptance_criteria.given: a refresh where the snapshot read fails
acceptance_criteria.then: the last-known data stays visible with an explicit 'snapshot stale' indicator

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The existing Hub monitor collection (aitri-hub monitor / FR-002..004, FR-007..009) keeps producing the same collected data after the redesign
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given a set of registered projects, when the collector runs post-redesign, then dashboard.json contains the same project fields (phases, verifyStatus, gitMeta, testSummary, alerts) it produced pre-redesign for the same inputs; a golden-snapshot diff shows no field regressions

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The dashboard.json snapshot contract (FR-009) remains valid and backward-compatible — the redesigned frontend reads it without requiring project-file access
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given a collection cycle, when dashboard.json is written, then it is valid JSON via atomic temp+rename, and the redesigned React app renders solely from it with zero direct project-file reads (verified by network/fs trace)

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Existing data ingestion from Aitri projects (.aitri state, 04_TEST_RESULTS.json, git metadata) still yields the same fields the redesign consumes
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given a project with a valid .aitri and test results, when collected, then currentPhase/approvedPhases/verifySummary/rejections/drift/testSummary are extracted identically to the pre-redesign baseline; a malformed .aitri still yields status 'unreadable' with no crash

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The redesigned UI meets its interaction and accessibility targets: time-to-urgency, render latency, contrast, non-color status encoding, and reduced-motion
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Time-to-urgency: the most urgent project is identifiable in <10s (largest+first+red). Each detail section renders in ≤2s for a project with ≤20 artifacts. All text/badges meet WCAG AA contrast ≥4.5:1. Every status is conveyed by icon+label, never color alone. Animations complete in ≤200ms and are suppressed under prefers-reduced-motion. Desktop breakpoints: ≥1200px 4-col, 768–1200px 2-col, no horizontal scroll ≥768px

non_functional_requirements.requirement: Manual-test evidence upload is validated and confined; QA write endpoints stay loopback-only
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given an evidence attachment, when uploaded, then only PNG/JPG/GIF/WebP/SVG under a fixed size cap (e.g. ≤5MB) are accepted — other types/sizes are rejected before persistence; evidence is written only under ~/.aitri-hub/ (path is whitelisted, never derived from client input; '..' and absolute paths rejected); any /api QA write from a non-loopback peer (not 127.0.0.1/::1) returns HTTP 403

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The Hub HTTP server logs every request
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: Given any HTTP request to the Hub server, when handled, then a line '[timestamp] METHOD /path STATUS' is written to stdout/stderr

non_functional_requirements.requirement: CI runs the full declared test suite (including any e2e runner) on every push to main
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: The CI workflow file triggers on push to main and invokes the project's full test command (unit + e2e) — asserted structurally on the workflow file (presence, trigger, exact test command)

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The Hub server exposes a healthcheck
non_functional_requirements.acceptance_criteria: GET /health returns HTTP 200 with a small JSON body while the process is alive

constraints: Inherits parent constraints: Node.js 18+ server, zero npm runtime dependencies for the server (Node built-ins only), all persistent data under ~/.aitri-hub/ (no writes to project dirs), admin/QA HTTP API loopback-only with no auth
Web stack: React + Vite bundled and served by the Node process (parent technology_preferences)
Design system per parent UI_UX_SPEC_V2.md: slate dark palette, Inter + JetBrains Mono typography — replacing the prototypes' synthwave look (prototypes are layout/interaction reference only)
Frontend reads only ~/.aitri-hub/dashboard.json (+ QA execution store under ~/.aitri-hub/) — no direct project-file access from React
Prototypes (aitri-hub-jarvis-health.jsx, aitri-hub-final.jsx) are visual/interaction references with mock data — not data-contract authorities
technology_preferences: Data contract, palette/type, and QA Workspace: follow parent UI_UX_SPEC_V2.md
Markdown/JSON/image rendering in the reader: client-side render from snapshot-provided artifact content

idea_provenance_sources.problem: feature_context/aitri-hub-ui-discovery.md §Problema (user-designated context, authored by César Eyes)
idea_provenance_sources.baseline: feature_context/aitri-hub-ui-discovery.md §Problema (current pain: per-repo, raw artifacts, terminal commands)
idea_provenance_sources.success_metric: feature_context/aitri-hub-ui-discovery.md §Objetivos de la interfaz (<10s time-to-urgency, one-screen context, QA in-Hub, name translation, visual render)
idea_provenance_sources.no_go_zone: feature_context/aitri-hub-ui-discovery.md §Fuera de alcance v1 + user chat decision to defer professionalization to a separate feature

coverage_map.need: Monitor bento grid, urgency-weighted card size
Worst-first ordering, re-sort on refresh
Filters ALL/CRITICAL/AT RISK/NOMINAL + topbar (radial, counts, ticker, clock)
Card content: name, health badge, segmented pipeline, 6 signal tiles, top issue, detail CTA
Project Detail two-column shell, fixed sidebar, single-page, browser-back
Sidebar: identity, branch/type, mini pipeline, nav badges, quick stats
Overview: description, phase pipeline (readable labels), metric tiles, test telemetry gauge
Health: five dimensions with OK/WARN/CRITICAL and remediation
Artifacts explorer tree grouped by phase, status glyphs, readable names
Artifact reader renders Markdown/JSON/embedded images inline
Embedded-image support: MD relative-path + data-URI, PNG/JPG/SVG/GIF/WebP
Sessions chronological timeline + last-session context
Alerts aggregation: health issues + external signals, explicit empty state
Artifact name translation, fixed mapping, features inherit prefix, technical name secondary
QA Test Cases view by phase/feature, filters, manual status editable
QA manual execution: result/notes/evidence/env, bound to verify-run, multiple runs
QA Bugs view from Aitri BUGS.json, filters, detail, parse-error surfaced
QA reports (project/feature/release), on-demand from snapshot, printable
Global periodic refresh + countdown ticker, no manual refresh
Explicit empty states across sections
Desktop-optimized responsive, accessibility (contrast, non-color status, reduced-motion)
Must-not-break: aitri-hub monitor collection
Must-not-break: dashboard.json snapshot contract
Must-not-break: Aitri project data ingestion parity
Evidence upload security + loopback-only QA writes
Request logging
CI runs full suite on push
Healthcheck endpoint
Role separation / differentiated access
Push/background notifications and toasts
Artifact editing from Hub
File export (PDF/Markdown)
Non-Aitri project support
Authentication / multi-tenant
Team/commit velocity, vulnerable-deps alerts, code-coverage-%, light theme, uptime
Professionalization: installable app, isolated runtime, architecture upgrade
Separate release entity / release CRUD
Manual refresh control

original_brief:
<!-- AGENT: if you fill this for the user, confirm the ground-truth fields
     (Problem / Why, Target Users, Success Criteria, Out of Scope) with them —
     do not silently infer. Mark anything you inferred as "[ASSUMPTION] …".
     Phase 1 records these as a provenance contract and blocks unconfirmed,
     untracked guesses on the highest-value inputs. -->

## Feature
Redesign de Aitri Hub v0.3.0 — rediseño completo de la interfaz web (Monitor + Detalle de Proyecto + módulo QA) siguiendo el discovery de UI/UX.

> **Requerimiento principal:** `feature_context/aitri-hub-ui-discovery (1).md`
> (PRD / Discovery Brief v1.0 — César Eyes, Julio 2026). Es la fuente de verdad de UI/UX.
> **Referencia visual:** `feature_context/aitri-hub-jarvis-health.jsx` y `feature_context/aitri-hub-final.jsx` (prototipos — fuente de verdad del "cómo se ve").
>
> **Alcance decidido:** este feature es UI/UX solamente. La profesionalización del Hub
> (instalable, entorno/runtime propio, upgrade de arquitectura) se aborda en un feature
> aparte y futuro (`aitri-hub-platform-upgrade`), con su propio discovery.

## Problem / Why
(Del discovery.) Un equipo que trabaja con múltiples proyectos Aitri no tiene un punto de observación unificado. Entender el estado real de un proyecto exige entrar a cada repo, revisar artefactos en raw y correr comandos en terminal. No escala, no es accesible a roles no técnicos, y no distingue visualmente un proyecto bloqueado de uno inactivo.

## Target Users
(Del discovery.) **Three Amigos** — Dev, Producto y QA — usando Hub simultáneamente, sin separación de roles ni vistas diferenciadas en esta versión. La distinción es de énfasis:
- **Dev:** monitorea avance técnico, detecta drift, retoma proyectos.
- **Producto:** sigue el pipeline de fases, revisa artefactos traducidos a lenguaje de producto, valida estado general.
- **QA:** herramienta de trabajo activa — registra pruebas, consulta casos, genera informes.

## New Behavior
El sistema debe...

**Monitor (Home)**
- Mostrar todos los proyectos en un bento grid donde el tamaño de la card refleja urgencia (CRITICAL = 2 columnas + glow/pulso, AT RISK = mediana, NOMINAL = compacta).
- Ordenar por defecto CRITICAL primero → NOMINAL al final; reordenar en cada refresh.
- En cada card: nombre legible, badge de salud, pipeline como barra segmentada, 6 tiles de señales (Tests, Drift, Verify, Commits pendientes, Señales externas, Rechazos), el issue más crítico en una línea, y acceso al detalle.
- Filtros ALL / CRITICAL / AT RISK / NOMINAL y topbar con resumen radial, conteos, ticker de sync y reloj.

**Detalle de Proyecto** (single-page, dos columnas: sidebar fijo + contenido scrolleable)
- **Overview:** descripción, pipeline fase a fase con estado visual y labels legibles, tiles de métricas, telemetría de tests con gauge.
- **Health:** 5 dimensiones (Pipeline, Tests, Code, Artifacts, Version), cada una con badge OK/WARN/CRITICAL y la acción de remediación del issue.
- **Artifacts:** árbol por fase + panel de lectura; render de Markdown formateado, JSON como vista estructurada (no raw), imágenes embebidas inline; acciones según estado (aprobar / rechazar con feedback / completar).
- **Sessions:** log cronológico de eventos del ciclo de vida + contexto de última sesión.
- **Alerts:** todos los issues activos + señales externas de tools, con estado vacío explícito.

**Módulo QA** (dentro del detalle de proyecto)
- Casos de prueba por fase/feature (desde artefactos de testing de Aitri), con filtros; los manuales editables desde Hub.
- Ejecución de pruebas manuales: resultado (pass/fail/blocked), notas, evidencia adjunta, ambiente; múltiples ejecuciones por caso en el tiempo.
- Bugs consumidos desde Aitri, con filtros y vista de detalle.
- Informes de QA (resumen de proyecto / por feature / release report), visualizados en Hub con vista imprimible desde navegador.

**Traducción de nombres de artefactos:** mostrar nombres de producto legibles (PRD, TRD, QA Plan, etc.) con el nombre técnico como referencia secundaria; misma lógica para artefactos de feature con prefijo.

**Comportamiento global:** refresh periódico con ticker (sin refresh manual en v1), estados vacíos explícitos, navegación single-page (back del browser: detalle → monitor), optimizado para desktop.

## Success Criteria
(Objetivos de la interfaz, del discovery.)
- Given el monitor, When el usuario lo abre, Then identifica el proyecto más urgente en < 10 segundos.
- Given un proyecto, When el usuario abre su detalle, Then responde "¿en qué estado quedó?" sin abrir otra herramienta.
- Given QA, When trabaja en Hub, Then puede registrar y consultar resultados de pruebas directamente.
- Given cualquier rol, When lee la UI, Then la terminología técnica de Aitri está traducida a lenguaje de producto legible.
- Given un artefacto con contenido visual, When se abre, Then se renderiza como imagen (no como texto raw).
- Given el Hub v0.3.0, When corre, Then el comportamiento existente v0.2.1 (monitoreo, snapshots) sigue funcionando.

## Touch Points
Modifica: toda la capa de UI del Hub (Monitor, Detalle, y el flujo de lectura de artefactos actual). Añade: módulo QA (casos de prueba, ejecución manual, bugs, informes), traducción de nombres, render visual de artefactos.

## Must Not Break (Regression Boundary)
- El monitoreo de proyectos por el Hub (`aitri-hub monitor`) sigue funcionando.
- La lectura/generación de snapshots y el resto del comportamiento v0.2.1 se preserva.
- La ingesta de datos desde proyectos Aitri (estado de fases, tests, artefactos) sigue produciendo los mismos datos que hoy alimentan la vista.

## Out of Scope
(Fuera de alcance v1, del discovery.)
- Separación de usuarios/roles con acceso diferenciado.
- Notificaciones push o alertas en background.
- Edición de artefactos desde Hub.
- Exportación a archivo (PDF/Markdown) — la impresión desde navegador cubre el caso.
- Soporte para proyectos que no usan Aitri.
- Autenticación o multi-tenant.
- **Profesionalización del Hub** (instalable, entorno/runtime propio, upgrade de arquitectura) → se difiere a un feature aparte (`aitri-hub-platform-upgrade`).

## Preguntas abiertas de Discovery (a resolver en Fase 1)
Las 6 preguntas de "Criterios de aceptación de Discovery" del PRD:
1. ¿Qué formato de artefacto soporta render de imágenes embebidas y qué tipos de imagen?
2. ¿El mapeo de nombre técnico → nombre de producto para artefactos de features es configurable o fijo?
3. ¿Cómo se asocia una ejecución de prueba manual a un release / iteración?
4. ¿Modelo de datos de bugs — viven en Aitri o en un sistema externo?
5. ¿El informe de QA se genera on-demand o hay un snapshot guardado?
6. ¿Qué define una "feature" — división del pipeline o concepto separado?
